subject: [PATCH v6 20/20] nfsd: add support to CB_NOTIFY for dir attribute changes

reviews.author: Chuck Lever
reviews.summary:
The reviewer noted that when a client requests dir attribute change notifications, the CB_NOTIFY event queue can overflow due to the reserved slot for the dir-attr-change entry, causing the delegation to be recalled without draining the queue and resulting in requeued callbacks.

Reviewer noted that when xdr_reserve_space() succeeds but nfsd4_encode_dir_attr_change() returns NULL, the reserved space for *maskp is not rolled back and *maskp remains uninitialized, potentially leading to a garbage value being transmitted.

Reviewer Chuck Lever noted that the function nfsd4_cb_notify_prepare() unconditionally dereferences a pointer to an export object (dp->dl_stid.sc_export) which may be freed concurrently by drop_stid_export(), leading to a use-after-free bug, and requested synchronization or alternative handling.

Reviewer noted that the if statement checks dl_notify_mask again, even though it was already checked by its sole caller, and suggested removing this redundant check.
reviews.raw_body:
When a client requests NOTIFY4_CHANGE_DIR_ATTRS, the CB_NOTIFY event
queue can fill to NOTIFY4_EVENT_QUEUE_SIZE (3) events while the consumer
only accepts 2 (it reserves a slot for the dir-attr-change entry). The
resulting overflow path in nfsd4_cb_notify_prepare() recalls the
delegation without draining the queue, and nfsd4_cb_notify_release()
then requeues the same callback indefinitely.

---

Nit:

When xdr_reserve_space() for maskp succeeds but nfsd4_encode_dir_attr_change()
returns NULL, the 4-byte reservation is never rolled back and *maskp is never
written, yet the function still takes the success path (return true). Unlike
the child-event loop, this branch does not escalate to error = true.

This is probably benign only because nfs4_xdr_enc_cb_notify re-encodes from
the ncn_nf[] array (and count was not incremented), so the garbage hole is
never transmitted.

---

The function dereferences the new exp parameter unconditionally.

The argument is dp->dl_stid.sc_export, read unlocked at
nfs4xdr.c:4297 and handed down. An in-flight CB_NOTIFY callback holds a
sc_count reference but NOT an export reference. drop_stid_export() can
run concurrently (admin revoke / unexport), NULL sc_export, and drop what
may be the last export reference, freeing the svc_export while the
callback dereferences it.

---

It looks like this if() re-checks dl_notify_mask even though its
sole caller already gated on the identical check.

nfsd4_encode_notify_event() does not repeat its caller's check.
The guard is unreachable from current callers.

2026-06-12.patch_summary: This patch adds support to the NFS server (nfsd) to send directory attribute change notifications to clients when requested. The client must have specifically asked for these notifications, and they are only sent in addition to other events such as add/remove/rename operations. If the export is configured to require signed filehandles, the server will append a MAC to the filehandle before sending it to the client. This ensures that the client can verify the filehandle's integrity.
